question: If a company's revenue doubles each year for 3 years starting from $1 million, and then decreases by 50% in the fourth year, is the final revenue greater than the starting revenue?
If all Bloops are Razzies and all Razzies are Lazzies, and some Lazzies are Dazzies, can we conclude that some Bloops might be Dazzies?
facts: Since Bloops are Lazzies, and some Lazzies are Dazzies, some Bloops might be Dazzies

question: A train travels at 60 mph. A car starts 120 miles behind the train and travels at 90 mph. Will the car catch up to the train within 3 hours?
facts: 4 hours > 3 hours, so no - wait, checking: Actually 4 > 3 so NOT within 3 hours

question: A train travels at 60 mph. A car starts 120 miles behind the train and travels at 90 mph. Will the car catch up to the train within 5 hours?
facts: 4 hours < 5 hours, so yes, car catches up within 5 hours

question: If a population of bacteria doubles every hour and starts with 1 bacterium, will there be more than 1000 bacteria after 10 hours?
In a tournament with 16 teams where each match eliminates one team, if Team A has played 3 matches and won all, and Team B has played 2 matches and won all, can both teams still be in the tournament?
facts: Team A won 3 matches → eliminated 3 opponents
Team B won 2 matches → eliminated 2 opponents
After 3 wins, Team A is in semi-finals (16→8→4→2)
After 2 wins, Team B is in quarter-finals
They would meet before Team B reaches Team A's level
So they cannot both still be undefeated in the tournament simultaneously

question: If you flip a fair coin 10 times and get heads all 10 times, is the probability of getting heads on the 11th flip still 50%?
facts: Past outcomes don't affect future outcomes
Probability remains 50% regardless of previous results

question: A rectangular garden is 20 meters long and 15 meters wide. If you increase both dimensions by 10%, will the area increase by more than 20%?
If it takes 5 machines 5 minutes to make 5 widgets, how long would it take 100 machines to make 100 widgets?
facts: This means 1 machine makes 1 widget in 5 minutes

question: A bat and ball cost $1.10 total. The bat costs $1 more than the ball. Is the ball's price $0.10?
If you have a 3-gallon jug and a 5-gallon jug, can you measure exactly 4 gallons?
facts: Pour from 5-gallon into 3-gallon (needs 1 gallon to fill)

question: In a room of 30 people, is it more likely than not that at least two people share the same birthday (assuming 365 days)?
facts: Birthday paradox: calculate probability all birthdays are different
70.6% > 50%, so yes, more likely than not

question: If a ladder is leaning against a wall at 45 degrees and the bottom is moved 1 foot away from the wall, will the top move down by exactly 1 foot?
facts: At 45 degrees, height = distance from wall (isosceles right triangle)
If ladder length = L, then height = L/√2 and distance = L/√2
Moving bottom by 1 foot: new distance = L/√2 + 1

question: If you travel halfway to work at 30 mph, how fast must you travel the rest of the way to average 60 mph for the entire trip?
facts: For average 60 mph over distance D
Total time must be D/60 hours
First half (D/2) at 30 mph: time = (D/2)/30 = D/60 hours
Impossible to average 60 mph - would need infinite speed

question: A snail climbs 3 feet up a wall during the day and slides down 2 feet at night. The wall is 10 feet tall. Will the snail reach the top in 8 days?
facts: Day 8: climbs to 10 - reaches the top!

question: If all houses in a street are numbered 1, 2, 3... and the sum of numbers on one side equals the sum on the other side, can there be exactly 10 houses?
facts: For equal sums, each side must sum to 55/2 = 27.5
Impossible to have equal sums with 10 houses

question: A clock shows 3:15. Is the angle between the hour and minute hands exactly 0 degrees?
facts: At 3:15, minute hand is at 90 degrees (pointing at 3)
Hour hand moves 30 degrees per hour
At 3:15, hour hand is at 90 + 7.5 = 97.5 degrees

question: If you roll two fair dice, is the probability of getting a sum of 7 higher than the probability of getting a sum of 12?
facts: 6 ways > 1 way, so yes, probability of 7 is higher

question: In a race, you pass the person in second place. Are you now in first place?
facts: You pass the person in second place
You take their position: second place
First place is still ahead of you

question: A farmer has 17 sheep. All but 9 die. How many sheep does the farmer have left?
If a medicine is 99% effective and you test positive, is there a 99% chance you have the disease?
facts: But P(disease|positive) depends on base rate

question: Is the sum of all integers from 1 to 100 greater than 5000?
If a car's speed increases from 60 mph to 80 mph, does the stopping distance increase by exactly 33%?
facts: Energy scales with square of speed

question: In a family with two children, if at least one is a boy, is the probability that both are boys exactly 50%?
facts: Possible combinations: BB, BG, GB, GG (4 equally likely)
Given at least one boy: BB, BG, GB (3 cases)

question: If you have a deck of 52 cards and draw 5 cards, is it more likely to get a flush (all same suit) than a straight (5 consecutive cards)?
facts: Number of possible straights: 10 × 4⁵ = 10,240 (minus straight flushes)
Straights are approximately twice as likely as flushes

question: A plane crashes on the border of two countries. In which country do they bury the survivors?
facts: Survivors are not buried - they survived

question: If you're running a race and overtake the last person, are you in last place?
facts: You cannot overtake the last person
If you're behind the last person, they're not last - you are

question: Does 0.999... (repeating) equal exactly 1?
If a pizza is cut into 8 equal slices and you eat 3 slices, have you eaten more than one-third of the pizza?
In a group of 6 people, is it guaranteed that either 3 people all know each other or 3 people are all strangers to each other?
facts: A has 5 relationships, so at least 3 are same type (friends or strangers)
If A has 3 friends: either two are friends (triangle) or all strangers

question: If you add 1 to a number and then subtract 1, is the result always the original number?
facts: Addition and subtraction are inverse operations
